standard.name: SD Impact Calculator
standard.description: Point-based criteria across Environmental, Social, Cultural, and Financial pillars.

criteria.label: Environmental Impact Reduction
criteria.summary: Actions lead to designs with a lower impact.
criteria.description: The project strategy includes at least one action to reduce environmental impact.

criteria.label: Product Ingredients List
criteria.summary: The display of “ingredients” or material components.
criteria.description: The product or product packaging includes a list of components and/or ingredients.
examples: Packaging that displays a list of material components

criteria.label: Ink, Printing, and Finishes
criteria.summary: Reduces ink coverage on printed materials.
criteria.description: The project materials were designed to reduce or eliminate the use of inks, varnishes and finishes or requires no printing.
examples: Packaging that displays a list of material components

criteria.label: Paper
criteria.summary: Efficient use of paper and reduces paper use.
criteria.description: The project materials were designed to reduce paper.
examples: A magazine reduces paper use year over year.

criteria.label: Product Quantity
criteria.description: No more units than needed were produced (including the possibility of no physical copies at all).
examples: A magazine that switches from print distribution to online distribution.

criteria.label: Extended Life Product
criteria.summary: Product design for longer life than the industry average.
criteria.description: When a longer lifetime is desirable for a product or system, it is designed to be reused, recharged, repaired, and/or upgraded as an environmental benefit.
examples: A mobile phone designed to be repaired.

criteria.label: Durability
criteria.summary: Physical durability as a product asset, where applicable.
criteria.description: The product or system is physically durable.
examples: A tablet that withstands damage when dropped accidentally.

criteria.label: Toxins
criteria.summary: Reduces or eliminates toxins in a product.
criteria.description: The project reduces or eliminates the use of toxins.
examples: A mural that uses low-VOC paint.

criteria.label: Environmental Policy
criteria.description: The designing entity and a majority of suppliers have a publicly-published environmental policy.

criteria.label: Lifecycle Assessment
criteria.summary: Design addresses the environmental impact of a product.
criteria.description: The project’s strategy is supported by a qualified, simplified life cycle study (LCS) or quantitative life cycle assessment (LCA).
